---
title: "Action Items & TODOs"
format: html
---

# Active Backlog

This page tracks only the currently active, unresolved work that should shape the next implementation passes.

- Historical resolved items now live in [Resolved TODOs](resolved-todos.qmd).
- `docs/typst/paper/main.typ` remains the highest-level project narrative.
- `.agents/memory/state/PROJECT_STATE.md` and `.agents/memory/state/OPEN_QUESTIONS.md` remain the canonical compact state surfaces outside the published docs.

## Codebase Simplification

### Current focus

- The main structural problem is duplicated ownership of the same contracts, especially across `aria_nbv.data` and `aria_nbv.data_handling`.
- Helper logic is still too often defined inside feature modules instead of living in shared helper owners.
- Plotting and app-panel code still carries too many repeated low-level helpers, which obscures the runtime architecture.

### Action items

- [ ] Collapse duplicated `aria_nbv.data` core modules into thin compatibility wrappers over `aria_nbv.data_handling`, then remove duplicate logic.
- [ ] Finish the two heavy compatibility collapses that still own real logic.
  - `aria_nbv.data.offline_cache` -> `aria_nbv.data_handling.oracle_cache`
  - `aria_nbv.data.vin_snippet_cache` -> `aria_nbv.data_handling.vin_cache`
- [ ] Keep every shared type/model contract in exactly one canonical owner.
  - Priority families: `Efm*View`, `VinSnippetView`, `AseEfmDataset*`, `VinOracleBatch`, `OracleRriCache*`, `VinSnippetCache*`, `MeshProcessSpec`, `ProcessedMesh`.
- [ ] Collapse cache/index/metadata helpers to one shared owner instead of redefining `_config_signature`, `_compute_config_hash`, `_ensure_config_hash`, `_read_metadata`, `_write_metadata`, and sample-key helpers across adjacent cache modules.
- [ ] Move reusable VIN snippet helpers into a shared helper module and stop duplicating `build_vin_snippet_view`, `empty_vin_snippet`, and cache-hash helpers across cache/provider modules.
- [ ] Consolidate duplicated plotting primitives into `aria_nbv.utils.plotting` and import them from feature modules instead of carrying local copies.
- [ ] Continue extracting the remaining duplicated VIN plotting helpers from `vin/plotting.py` and `vin/experimental/plotting.py`.
  - Current high-overlap helpers still duplicated there: pose conversion/broadcast helpers, frustum builders, voxel/world helpers, and 3D trace helpers.
- [ ] Reduce helper-heavy modules by extracting pure helper logic into explicit shared helper modules.
  - Highest-yield targets: `vin/plotting.py`, `vin/experimental/plotting.py`, `app/panels/optuna_sweep.py`, `data_handling/_offline_writer.py`.
- [ ] Remove duplicated experimental VIN model branches or make one explicitly canonical.
  - Current unresolved pair: `vin/experimental/model.py` vs `vin/experimental/model_v1_SH.py`.
- [ ] Collapse repeated app/session cache boilerplate into shared helpers or generic owners instead of bespoke one-field dataclasses and panel-local utilities.
- [ ] Keep moving generic numeric/statistical helpers out of feature modules and into shared utility owners.
  - Remaining likely seams after the latest pass: app/W&B trend helpers beyond slope segmentation, plus any repeated smoothing or aggregation helpers that appear in analysis panels.
- [ ] Continue the `autoimprove` loop each pass: reduce LOC and moving parts, verify behavior, and refresh the redundancy report after meaningful simplification work.

## Data Handling & Offline Dataset

### Current focus

- `aria_nbv.data_handling` should become the single owner of the raw ASE/EFM snippet stack and the offline VIN data path.
- The new immutable offline format should stay optimized for multi-worker random-access loading.
- Future multi-step work should extend the same offline family instead of creating new parallel dataset families.

### Action items

- [ ] Finish migrating active training and app contact points to the root `aria_nbv.data_handling` API.
- [ ] Keep only one offline VIN dataset concept.
  - Legacy oracle/VIN caches may remain readable during migration, but should not remain peer architectures.
- [ ] Finish the storage simplification.
  - Keep hot-path numeric blocks on the immutable zarr-backed store and remove remaining legacy-style sidecar serialization where possible.
- [ ] Simplify offline metadata so one normalized manifest describes source config, oracle config, materialized blocks, VIN settings, provenance, and split/shard statistics.
- [ ] Design the offline schema so it can optionally materialize `K` counterfactual trajectories per snippet without introducing a third dataset family.
- [ ] Verify that offline reads remain correct for batched training, app diagnostics, and multi-worker random access after each simplification pass.

## VIN Training & Evaluation

### Current focus

- The current VIN path is useful but still exploratory; calibration, candidate-specific evidence, and data-path correctness remain the main concerns.

### Action items

- [ ] Shuffle candidates and target RRIs before each training step to eliminate ordering bias.
- [ ] Make the online oracle-labeling datamodule path reliable with `num_workers > 0`.
- [ ] Add stronger Lightning-side metrics beyond loss, including explicit ranking/calibration diagnostics.
- [ ] Add a strict frame-consistency check for VIN inputs so candidate poses, reference poses, and `p3d_cameras` cannot silently drift into different coordinate conventions.
- [ ] Decide which experimental VIN model/plotting surfaces are still live, then merge or delete the redundant ones.

## Streamlit & Diagnostics

### Current focus

- The Streamlit app is the main glass-box surface for validating the oracle and VIN pipelines, so stale legacy imports and duplicated helpers are especially costly here.

### Action items

- [ ] Migrate app panels away from legacy `aria_nbv.data` imports for core data contracts.
- [ ] Collapse repeated panel-level helper/dataclass patterns into shared app helper/state owners where that reduces boilerplate.
- [ ] Keep offline-diagnostics pages aligned with the new `data_handling` / immutable offline dataset flow.

## Research-Facing Next Steps

### Current focus

- The project still centers on one-step discrete candidate ranking. The next major research step is extending that supervision into richer counterfactual trajectory data.

### Action items

- [ ] Specify how the offline dataset should represent optional multi-step / counterfactual trajectory supervision for future RL-style experiments.
- [ ] Decide which counterfactual modalities are worth materializing offline versus deriving from GT mesh or other geometry at train time.
- [ ] Quantify which current VIN bottlenecks are architectural versus data/supervision bottlenecks before committing to a larger model rewrite.
